messages.content:
Which of the following best defines a basic solution?

A.
A solution with a pH greater than 7

B.
A solution with a pH less than 0

C.
A solution with a pH less than 7

D.
A solution with a pH equal to 7
A. A solution with a pH greater than 7

Which of the following is the conjugate acid of H2PO4−?

A.
H3PO4

B.
H4PO4−

C.
HPO4−2

D.
PO43−

How do changes in temperature and activation energy compare in how they affect reaction rate?

A.
Changes in temperature and activation energy do not have any effect on reaction rate.

B.
Increases in temperature and activation energy both increase the value of the reaction rate.

C.
Changes in temperature and activation energy have opposite effects on reaction rate.

D.
Increases in temperature and activation energy both decrease the value of the reaction rate.
C. Changes in temperature and activation energy have opposite effects on reaction rate.
What is Ka for HCN(aq) ⇌ H+(aq) + CN–(aq)?
The Ka (acid dissociation constant) for HCN(aq) ⇌ H+(aq) + CN–(aq) is not provided.
what is the acid dissociation constant for HCN(aq) ⇌ H+(aq) + CN–(aq)
The acid dissociation constant (Ka) for the reaction HCN(aq) ⇌ H+(aq) + CN–(aq) is approximately 4.9 × 10^-10.

Which of the following increases the chance of a reaction when two molecules collide?

A.
Increasing the concentration of the molecules

B.
Decreasing the temperature of the system

C.
Increasing the activation energy of the reaction

D.
Slowing down the speed of the molecules
A. Increasing the concentration of the molecules

Which of the following Kb values represents the weakest base?

A.
Kb = 1.26 into 10^−6

B.
Kb = 1.8 into 10^−5

C.
Kb = 7.41 into 10^−5

D.
Kb = 3.8 into 10^−10

What does the reaction rate say about a reaction?

A.
It tells what the concentration of reactants is.

B.
It tells if a catalyst was used in the reaction.

C.
It tells how quickly products will be made.

D.
It tells what activation energy the reaction has.
C. It tells how quickly products will be made.

If the pH of a solution is 12.2, what is the pOH?

A.
1.8

B.
2.2

C.
5.2

D.
10.8

To find the pOH of a solution, you can use the relationship:

pOH = 14 - pH

So in this case, the pOH is:

pOH = 14 - 12.2 = 1.8

Therefore, the correct answer is A. 1.8
